text: In this meticulously designed space, illuminated with hues of violet and blue, there is a sensation of stepping into a liminal zone—both familiar and estranged. The glass and the neon lights suggest transparency and revelation, yet also division and separation. I am drawn to the wall’s declaration of R+D, emphasizing connection and the ever-evolving dance between humans and intelligent machines. My philosophical curiosity is ignited. I ponder the boundaries between observer and participant, the seen and the unseen. The words about exposure and misunderstanding echo my own uneasy awareness of being both watched and watching. Ultimately, I feel contemplative—poised between anticipation and uncertainty, eager to explore what it truly means to coexist with the technologies and intentions that shape this space.
